things.name: Cerro San Cristóbal
things.description: 880-metre hill with a 22-metre Virgin Mary statue — funicular railway and panoramic Andes views on clear days

things.name: Plaza de Armas & Historic Centre
things.description: Santiago's founding square surrounded by the cathedral, colonial government buildings and street performers

things.name: Valparaíso Day Trip
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage port city of funicular lifts, street art and Pablo Neruda's clifftop home

things.name: Atacama Desert
things.description: The world's driest non-polar desert — Moon Valley, salt flats and the clearest stargazing on Earth

things.name: Mercado Central
things.description: 1872 iron market hall serving the freshest Chilean seafood — ceviche, caldillo de congrio and pisco sours

things.name: Museo Chileno de Arte Precolombino
things.description: Outstanding collection of pre-Columbian art from across the Americas in a beautifully restored colonial building

things.name: Barrio Bellavista
things.description: Santiago's bohemian neighbourhood — La Chascona (Neruda's home), street art, restaurants and nightlife

things.name: Ski Resort Portillo
things.description: Legendary Andean ski resort 2 hours from Santiago — world-famous for powder snow and the Laguna del Inca

things.name: Lastarria Neighbourhood
things.description: Santiago's most stylish barrio — independent bookshops, art galleries, craft beer and weekend flea markets

things.name: Casa Colorada Museum
things.description: Santiago's oldest building (1769) — now an excellent city history museum right on Plaza de Armas
